name: How long does window installation take in coastal San Mateo County homes?

mainEntity.name: How long does window installation usually take for a typical home in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, or Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: How long does window installation usually take for a typical home in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, or Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most residential window installation projects in coastal San Mateo County can be completed in one to two days for a typical retrofit job, while full-frame replacement or homes with rot, water damage, or structural repairs can take several days. The biggest factors are the number of windows, the installation method, the condition of the existing openings, and whether the crew needs to repair flashing, trim, or exterior siding before setting new units. In places like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay, wind and moisture can slow scheduling and require extra weatherproofing steps. If the project involves custom sizes or specialty glass, ordering time may be longer than the installation itself. Homeowners should ask for a written schedule that separates measurement, product lead time, installation day, and finish work so there are no surprises. A good installer will also explain whether you can keep living in the house during the work and which rooms will be affected each day.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most window installation projects take one to two days for a standard home, but the timeline depends on the number of windows, whether they are full-frame replacements, and how much trim or siding repair is needed. Coastal weather, access, and permit requirements can also add time.
